commit.message:
refactor: move granian config and PodMonitor into OLApplicationK8s component (#4272)

* refactor: move granian config and PodMonitor into OLApplicationK8s component

Add GranianConfig Pydantic model to k8s.py that encapsulates all
granian server options (interface, workers, runtime_mode, runtime_threads,
no_ws, workers_max_rss, blocking_threads_idle_timeout, respawn_failed_workers,
backlog, log_level, application_module, enable_metrics, metrics_port,
metrics_scrape_interval, nginx_config_filename) along with a build_args()
method that produces the full CLI argument list.

OLApplicationK8sConfig gains a granian_config field. When set, OLApplicationK8s:
- builds cmd/args from GranianConfig.build_args() instead of application_cmd_array
- selects files/<nginx_config_filename> for the nginx ConfigMap
- exposes a named 'metrics' ContainerPort when enable_metrics=True
- creates a PodMonitor CustomResource when enable_metrics=True

Update mitxonline, mit_learn, and learn_ai to pass granian_config instead
of manually constructing cmd_array/arg_array/nginx_config_path conditionals
and creating standalone PodMonitor resources. Each caller now has a single
GranianConfig(...) if config.get_bool('use_granian') else None expression.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: set nginx_config_filename='web.conf' for learn_ai GranianConfig

learn_ai only has files/web.conf and never had a separate granian-specific
nginx config. The previous code left import_nginx_config_path unset so the
component always read files/web.conf. After the refactoring, GranianConfig
defaulted to nginx_config_filename='web.conf_granian', causing a
FileNotFoundError on deployment since that file doesn't exist.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* review: address PR feedback on GranianConfig and PodMonitor

- Add host/port fields to GranianConfig and use them in build_args()
  instead of hard-coding 0.0.0.0 and DEFAULT_WSGI_PORT
- Add field_validator on nginx_config_filename to reject values
  containing path separators or '..' (prevent path traversal)
- Apply truncate_k8s_metanames() to PodMonitor logical name and
  metadata.name to handle long application names safely
- Use the already-computed application_labels dict for the PodMonitor
  selector instead of duplicating label keys inline
- Update GranianConfig docstring to clarify the supported option subset

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add cross-model validators for port/nginx coupling and duplicate metrics port

- validate_granian_port_nginx_coupling: raises ValueError when granian_config.port
  differs from DEFAULT_WSGI_PORT but nginx_config_filename is still the default
  'web.conf_granian'. The default nginx config proxies to DEFAULT_WSGI_PORT so an
  overridden port would silently misdirect all traffic.
- validate_no_duplicate_metrics_port: raises ValueError when
  granian_config.enable_metrics=True and extra_container_ports already contains a
  port named 'metrics', which would cause Kubernetes to reject the pod spec.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Align GranianConfig defaults with mitxonline best practices

- Add memory_limit field: callers pass a Kubernetes memory limit string
  (e.g. '1500Mi') and workers_max_rss is computed automatically as
  floor(memory_limit / workers * 0.9) MiB, replacing the manual
  parse_quantity calculation that was duplicated in mitxonline.
- Default respawn_failed_workers to True (was False).
- Add parse_quantity import to k8s.py (now needed by GranianConfig).
- Simplify mitxonline GranianConfig call to use memory_limit= and remove
  the manual granian_workers_max_rss computation and parse_quantity import.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Drive workers_max_rss from container resource_limits, add limit_workers_max_rss toggle

- Remove memory_limit from GranianConfig; the component now reads
  resource_limits["memory"] from OLApplicationK8sConfig directly, keeping
  the memory budget in one place.
- Add limit_workers_max_rss: bool = True to GranianConfig. When True and
  workers_max_rss is None, the component auto-computes the cap using
  floor(memory_limit_bytes / workers * 0.9) MiB. Set to False to disable
  the --workers-max-rss flag entirely (e.g. ASGI apps without a hard budget).
- Simplify mitxonline GranianConfig: drop memory_limit= kwarg; the value is
  already present in resource_limits={"memory": mitxonline_web_memory_limit}.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Make workers=2 explicit in all GranianConfig callers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix workers_max_rss floor clamping and learn_ai nginx ConfigMap collision

- Clamp auto-computed workers_max_rss to max(1, ...) before passing to
  model_copy. Bypassing Pydantic validation via model_copy means a 0 MiB
  result (possible for small memory limits or high worker counts) would be
  passed directly to --workers-max-rss as an invalid argument. The clamp
  ensures at least 1 MiB is always emitted.
- Remove the standalone learn_ai_nginx_configmap resource. It created a
  ConfigMap with metadata.name='nginx-config' in the same namespace;
  OLApplicationK8s(import_nginx_config=True) creates an identically-named
  ConfigMap, which would cause a Kubernetes name collision and Pulumi
  would attempt to manage the same object twice. The component's ConfigMap
  reads from files/web.conf (via nginx_config_filename='web.conf') which
  is the same source, so no content is lost.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(superset): implement data governance RBAC and row-level security (#4235)

* feat(superset): implement data governance RBAC and RLS policies

Aligns Superset with the OL data governance framework by introducing
role-based access control and row-level security for all six Keycloak
roles.

Changes:
- ol_governance_roles.json: FAB-compatible role definitions for
  ol_instructor, ol_data_analyst, ol_business_analyst, ol_researcher,
  and ol_data_engineer. ol_platform_admin maps to the built-in Admin
  role.
- ol_rls_policies.json: Three RLS filters grounded in the actual
  registered Superset/Trino datasets. ol_instructor is restricted to
  courses where they hold an 'instructor' or 'staff' enrollment mode
  (looked up via marts__combined_course_enrollment_detail), with a
  combined 30-day post-term expiry on enrollment-detail tables.
  A Base filter exempts Admin and ol_data_engineer from all
  course-scope restrictions.
- apply_rls_policies.py: Post-deploy script that waits for Superset
  to be healthy, authenticates via the REST API, resolves role and
  dataset names to IDs, and idempotently creates or updates each RLS
  filter.
- superset_config.py: AUTH_ROLES_MAPPING updated to cover all six
  Keycloak roles. Previously ol_instructor and ol_business_analyst
  were missing and fell through to the Public role.
- __main__.py: Mounts policy files into /app/pythonpath via
  extraConfigs, overrides init.command to run flask fab import-roles
  after superset init, adds a Vault-synced admin credential secret,
  and adds a Kubernetes Batch Job that applies RLS filters
  post-deployment.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(superset): address PR review feedback on governance RBAC and RLS

- Fix non-deterministic hash(): replace built-in hash() with
  hashlib.sha256 over policy JSON + script bytes so the Job annotation
  only changes when content changes.
- Fix Job file mounting: policy files are no longer embedded in the
  Helm chart extraConfigs (which only reaches Helm-managed pods).
  A dedicated superset-rls-policies ConfigMap is created and mounted
  as a volume at /app/rls-policies/ in the Job pod.
- Fix RLS clause: current_username() returns Keycloak preferred_username,
  not email. Changed to current_email() which returns the user's email
  from the JWT, matching the user_email column in enrollment tables.
- Fix superset_config.py comment: update stale reference to old
  Concourse path.
- Fix dataset lookup: index datasets by both bare table_name and
  schema.table_name so policy files can use either format.
- Fix role/table missing handling: apply_rls_filter now raises
  RuntimeError for missing roles or tables (was silently returning/
  partially applying). Consistent behaviour for both, and the Job's
  backoff_limit=3 ensures automatic retry.
- Fix main loop: track successes and failures; exit with non-zero
  status if any policy fails so Kubernetes retries the Job.
- Fix init command: add set -e so any failure in the init sequence
  (bootstrap, db upgrade, superset init, import-roles) aborts cleanly.
- Fix docstring: wait_for_superset used 'exponential' but implemented
  linear backoff.
- Add active_deadline_seconds=600 to JobSpec.
- Validate ADMIN_PASSWORD is non-empty at startup; fail fast with a
  clear error rather than a cryptic auth failure.
- Update ol_rls_policies.json _comment to clarify bare vs schema-
  qualified table name format.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(superset): align RLS queries with production query pattern

Use the same table, column, and Jinja macro as the existing manually
created RLS filter:

- Table: ol_data_lake_production.ol_warehouse_production_intermediate
  .int__combined__user_course_roles (cross-platform course roles)
- Column: courseaccess_role (not courserunenrollment_enrollment_mode)
- Macro: current_user_email() (not current_email())

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(superset): address PR review feedback round 2

- Remove shebang from apply_rls_policies.py (EXE001 pre-commit error;
  file is executed via 'python3 ...' in the K8s Job, not directly)
- Increase active_deadline_seconds from 600 to 1800 to exceed the
  worst-case wait_for_superset ceiling of ~825s (MAX_RETRIES=10,
  RETRY_DELAY=15s, sum = 825s)
- Fix dataset collision detection in get_dataset_name_to_id: bare
  table_name key is now removed when two datasets share the same name
  across schemas, forcing policies to use the qualified schema.table_name
  form; a warning is logged to make collisions visible
- Add _expand_role_keys() helper and apply it in
  _get_roles_from_keycloak_roles() so the JWT auth path runs role keys
  through AUTH_ROLES_MAPPING consistently with the OAuth path (e.g.
  ol_platform_admin -> Admin)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(superset): add SQL Lab permissions to ol_researcher role

The governance document specifies that ol_researcher has CREATE TABLE AS
SELECT capability in a dedicated research workspace and full SELECT
access across Silver/Gold Analytics tiers. SQL Lab is required in
Superset to support this access pattern.

Without these permissions, existing researcher users would lose SQL Lab
access when the new role definitions are applied.

Permissions added per reviewer suggestion (quazi-h):
- can_get_results, can_execute_sql_query, can_estimate_query_cost,
  can_format_sql, can_export_csv, can_read on SQLLab
- can_read, can_write on SqlLabPermalinkRestApi
- menu_access on SQL Lab and SQL Editor

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: eval(), generator throw, shape IC, string concat optimization (#455)

* Wire shape-based inline cache to property access hot path

Add shape_id to PropertyMap that changes on structural mutations (property
add/remove, attribute changes) but stays stable on value-only updates.
Expose offset_of(), get_by_offset(), set_by_offset(), and
is_writable_by_offset() for direct Vec-indexed O(1) property access.

Introduce PropertyIc struct (cached_shape, cached_offset) and add
shape_load_ic / shape_store_ic HashMaps to InterpreterFrame.

Wire the shape IC into LdaNamedProperty and StaNamedProperty handlers:
- Load: shape IC tried first (before poly cache); on miss, populate IC
  for own-property hits on PlainObject.
- Store: shape IC fast-path for existing writable properties; on miss,
  populate IC after slow-path store completes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Sprint 33 Tasks 1+2: Sub.prefix, Kitty Keyboard Protocol CSI-u parsing (22 new tests, 1722 total)

- Sub.prefix: namespace prefix for TimerSub/CustomSub IDs prevents subscription
  ID collisions in multi-component apps; identity on KeySub/MouseSub/etc.
- Kitty keyboard protocol parser (parseKittyKey):
  - CSI-u format: ESC [ <codepoint> [; <modifiers> [: <event-type>]] u
  - Handles press (event-type=1), repeat (2, dispatched as KeyPressed), release (3, None)
  - Maps special codepoints: 9=Tab, 13=Enter, 27=Escape, 127=Backspace
  - Unambiguous Shift+Enter, Ctrl+Space, extended modifier combos
  - isCompleteEscSeq already handles 'u' final byte (in [@,~] range)
  - Legacy CSI sequences ([A, [1;5A, etc.) still parse correctly
  - 17 Kitty protocol tests + 5 Sub.prefix tests

commit.message:
Add currentRole to Health Summary Report at /health (#3158)

## Why make this change?

Adds `currentRole` to the `/health` endpoint response to surface the
effective role of the requesting user, enabling easier authentication
testing without needing external tooling to inspect request headers.

## What is this change?

- **`ComprehensiveHealthCheckReport`**: New `currentRole` string
property (JSON: `"currentRole"`) inserted after `timestamp` in the
report header.
- **`HealthCheckHelper.ReadRoleHeaders(HttpContext)`**: Replaces the
former `StoreIncomingRoleHeader` method. Instead of writing to mutable
instance fields on the singleton, it returns a `(roleHeader, roleToken)`
tuple that the caller holds as request-local values, eliminating a race
condition under concurrent requests.
- **`HealthCheckHelper.GetCurrentRole(roleHeader, roleToken)`**:
Determines the effective role using a three-way fallback: explicit
`X-MS-API-ROLE` header → `"authenticated"` (if a bearer token is present
via `X-MS-CLIENT-PRINCIPAL`) → `"anonymous"`. This correctly handles
authenticated users who provide a bearer token without an explicit role
header.
- **`HealthCheckHelper.IsUserAllowedToAccessHealthCheck` /
`GetHealthCheckResponseAsync`**: Updated to accept
`roleHeader`/`roleToken` as explicit parameters; the values flow down
the entire private call chain (`UpdateHealthCheckDetailsAsync` →
`UpdateEntityHealthCheckResultsAsync` → `PopulateEntityHealthAsync` →
`ExecuteRestEntityQueryAsync` / `ExecuteGraphQlEntityQueryAsync`) so no
per-request state is stored on the singleton.
- **`ComprehensiveHealthReportResponseWriter`**: Reads role headers once
per request into locals, passes them through all downstream calls, and
stamps `currentRole` per-request **after** cache retrieval (using a
non-destructive `with` expression) so cached responses never leak one
caller's role to another request. The cache now stores the
`ComprehensiveHealthCheckReport` object (without `currentRole`) rather
than a serialized string.

> **Access control note**: `IsUserAllowedToAccessHealthCheck` checks
`allowedRoles` against the explicit `X-MS-API-ROLE` header value only —
matching DAB's existing authorization policy where clients must
explicitly claim a role via the header.

Example response shape:
```json
{
  "status": "Healthy",
  "version": "1.x.x",
  "app-name": "dab_oss_1.x.x",
  "timestamp": "2026-02-24T22:28:30Z",
  "currentRole": "authenticated",
  "configuration": { ... },
  "checks": [ ... ]
}
```

## How was this tested?

- [ ] Integration Tests
- [x] Unit Tests

Unit tests added to `HealthCheckUtilitiesUnitTests.cs` covering all key
scenarios:
- `GetCurrentRole_NoHeaders_ReturnsAnonymous` — no auth headers →
`anonymous`
- `GetCurrentRole_BearerTokenOnly_ReturnsAuthenticated` — bearer token,
no role header → `authenticated`
- `GetCurrentRole_ExplicitRoleHeader_ReturnsHeaderValue` — explicit
`X-MS-API-ROLE` → exact header value
- `GetCurrentRole_BothHeaderAndToken_RoleHeaderWins` — role header takes
priority over bearer token
-
`GetCurrentRole_CacheDoesNotLeakRole_DifferentCallersGetDifferentRoles`
— sequential calls with different inputs each return the correct role,
confirming no cross-request state
- `GetCurrentRole_ParallelRequests_NoRoleBleed` — 50 concurrent tasks
each with a unique role return exactly their own role with no bleed
- `ReadRoleHeaders_WithRoleHeader_ReturnsRoleHeader` /
`ReadRoleHeaders_NoHeaders_ReturnsEmpty` — validates header extraction

## Sample Request(s)

```http
GET /health
X-MS-API-ROLE: myrole
```
Response includes `"currentRole": "myrole"`.

```http
GET /health
X-MS-CLIENT-PRINCIPAL: <base64-token>
```
Response includes `"currentRole": "authenticated"` (bearer token
present, no explicit role header).

```http
GET /health
```
Response includes `"currentRole": "anonymous"` (no role header, no
bearer token).

  thread history from the server before posting. Added read_thread(),
  get_my_mentions(), list_threads(), format_thread_history(). Server
  is the single source of truth for all conversation state.

  who said what when reading threads).

think, then respond. Conversations are resumable and new agents
can join at any time.

It enables owners to list and manage warehouses, while merchants can search, filter, and send inquiries — all powered by Next.js and Firebase.
